briefdescription.para: Free a string if it is not owned by the "dict" dictionary in the current scope.
Moves the position of the current instance to the next node in the stream, exposing its properties.

simplesect.para: 1 if the node was read successfully, 0 if there is no more nodes to read, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free a node, this is a recursive behaviour, all the children are freed too.

detaileddescription.para: This doesn't unlink the child from the list, use xmlUnlinkNode first.

briefdescription.para: Free a node and all its siblings, this is a recursive behaviour, all the children are freed too.

parameterdescription.para: the first node in the list

briefdescription.para: Free a node.
Free a property and all its siblings, all the children are freed too.

parameterdescription.para: the first property in the list

briefdescription.para: Free up all the structures used by a document, tree included.
Pushes a new entity reference node on top of the entities stack.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, the index in the stack otherwise

briefdescription.para: Pops the top element entity from the entities stack.

simplesect.para: the entity just removed

briefdescription.para: called when an opening tag has been processed.

parameterdescription.para: The element name, including namespace prefix
An array of name/value attributes pairs, NULL terminated

briefdescription.para: called when an ending tag has been processed.

parameterdescription.para: The element name, including namespace prefix

memberdef.argsstring: (void *ctx, const xmlChar *localname, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI, int nb_namespaces, const xmlChar **namespaces, int nb_attributes, int nb_defaulted, const xmlChar **attributes)
memberdef.name: xmlTextReaderStartElementNs

briefdescription.para: called when an opening tag has been processed.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the element
the element namespace prefix if available
the element namespace name if available
number of namespace definitions on that node
pointer to the array of prefix/URI pairs namespace definitions
the number of attributes on that node
pointer to the array of (localname/prefix/URI/value/end) attribute values.

memberdef.argsstring: (void *ctx, const xmlChar *localname, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI)
memberdef.name: xmlTextReaderEndElementNs

briefdescription.para: called when an ending tag has been processed.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the element
the element namespace prefix if available
the element namespace name if available

briefdescription.para: receiving some chars from the parser.
called when a pcdata block has been parsed
Push data down the progressive parser until a significant callback got raised.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of failure, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Push the current node for validation.
Push some CData for validation.

parameterdescription.para: length of the CData block in bytes.

briefdescription.para: Pop the current node from validation.
Handle the validation when an entity reference is encountered and entity substitution is not activated.

detaileddescription.para: As a result the parser interface must walk through the entity and do the validation calls

briefdescription.para: Get the successor of a node if available.

simplesect.para: the successor node or NULL

briefdescription.para: Makes sure that the current node is fully read as well as all its descendant.

detaileddescription.para: It means the full DOM subtree must be available at the end of the call.

simplesect.para: 1 if the node was expanded successfully, 0 if there is no more nodes to read, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the next node in the stream, exposing its properties.

simplesect.para: 1 if the node was read successfully, 0 if there is no more nodes to read, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Gets the read state of the reader.

simplesect.para: the state value, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Reads the contents of the current node and the full subtree.

detaileddescription.para: It then makes the subtree available until the next xmlTextReaderRead call

simplesect.para: a node pointer valid until the next xmlTextReaderRead call or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Skip to the node following the current one in document order while avoiding the subtree if any.

simplesect.para: 1 if the node was read successfully, 0 if there is no more nodes to read, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Reads the contents of the current node, including child nodes and markup.

simplesect.para: a string containing the XML content, or NULL if the current node is neither an element nor attribute, or has no child nodes. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Reads the contents of the current node, including child nodes and markup.

simplesect.para: a string containing the node and any XML content, or NULL if the current node cannot be serialized. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Reads the contents of an element or a text node as a string.

simplesect.para: a string containing the contents of the non-empty Element or Text node (including CDATA sections), or NULL if the reader is positioned on any other type of node. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Skip to the node following the current one in document order while avoiding the subtree if any.

detaileddescription.para: Currently implemented only for Readers built on a document

simplesect.para: 1 if the node was read successfully, 0 if there is no more nodes to read, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Create an xmlTextReader structure fed with input

parameterdescription.para: the xmlParserInputBuffer used to read data
the URI information for the source if available

simplesect.para: the new xmlTextReader or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Create an xmlTextReader structure fed with the resource at URI

parameterdescription.para: the URI of the resource to process

simplesect.para: the new xmlTextReader or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Deallocate all the resources associated to the reader.
This method releases any resources allocated by the current instance changes the state to Closed and close any underlying input.

simplesect.para: 0 or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Provides the value of the attribute with the specified index relative to the containing element.

parameterdescription.para: the zero-based index of the attribute relative to the containing element

simplesect.para: a string containing the value of the specified attribute, or NULL in case of error. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Provides the value of the attribute with the specified qualified name.

parameterdescription.para: the qualified name of the attribute.

simplesect.para: a string containing the value of the specified attribute, or NULL in case of error. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Provides the value of the specified attribute.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the attribute.
the namespace URI of the attribute.

simplesect.para: a string containing the value of the specified attribute, or NULL in case of error. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Method to get the remainder of the buffered XML.

detaileddescription.para: this method stops the parser, set its state to End Of File and return the input stream with what is left that the parser did not use.
The implementation is not good, the parser certainly progressed past what's left in reader->input, and there is an allocation problem. Best would be to rewrite it differently.

simplesect.para: the xmlParserInputBuffer attached to the XML or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Resolves a namespace prefix in the scope of the current element.

parameterdescription.para: the prefix whose namespace URI is to be resolved. To return the default namespace, specify NULL

simplesect.para: a string containing the namespace URI to which the prefix maps or NULL in case of error. The string must be deallocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the attribute with the specified index relative to the containing element.

parameterdescription.para: the zero-based index of the attribute relative to the containing element.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, -1 in case of error, 0 if not found

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the attribute with the specified qualified name.

parameterdescription.para: the qualified name of the attribute.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, -1 in case of error, 0 if not found

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the attribute with the specified local name and namespace URI.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the attribute.
the namespace URI of the attribute.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, -1 in case of error, 0 if not found

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the first attribute associated with the current node.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, -1 in case of error, 0 if not found

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the next attribute associated with the current node.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, -1 in case of error, 0 if not found

briefdescription.para: Moves the position of the current instance to the node that contains the current Attribute node.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, -1 in case of error, 0 if not moved

briefdescription.para: Parses an attribute value into one or more Text and EntityReference nodes.

simplesect.para: 1 in case of success, 0 if the reader was not positioned on an attribute node or all the attribute values have been read, or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Determine the encoding of the document being read.

simplesect.para: a string containing the encoding of the document or NULL in case of error. The string is deallocated with the reader.

briefdescription.para: Provides the number of attributes of the current node.

simplesect.para: 0 i no attributes, -1 in case of error or the attribute count

briefdescription.para: Get the node type of the current node Reference: http://www.gnu.org/software/dotgnu/pnetlib-doc/System/Xml/XmlNodeType.html.

simplesect.para: the xmlReaderTypes of the current node or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Check if the current node is empty.

simplesect.para: 1 if empty, 0 if not and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: The local name of the node.

simplesect.para: the local name or NULL if not available, if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: The local name of the node.

simplesect.para: the local name or NULL if not available, the string will be deallocated with the reader.

briefdescription.para: The qualified name of the node, equal to Prefix :LocalName.

simplesect.para: the local name or NULL if not available, if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: The qualified name of the node, equal to Prefix :LocalName.

simplesect.para: the local name or NULL if not available, the string is deallocated with the reader.

briefdescription.para: A shorthand reference to the namespace associated with the node.

simplesect.para: the prefix or NULL if not available, if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: A shorthand reference to the namespace associated with the node.

simplesect.para: the prefix or NULL if not available, the string is deallocated with the reader.

briefdescription.para: The URI defining the namespace associated with the node.

simplesect.para: the namespace URI or NULL if not available, if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: The URI defining the namespace associated with the node.

simplesect.para: the namespace URI or NULL if not available, the string will be deallocated with the reader

briefdescription.para: The base URI of the node.

simplesect.para: the base URI or NULL if not available, if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: The base URI of the node.

simplesect.para: the base URI or NULL if not available, the string will be deallocated with the reader

briefdescription.para: The depth of the node in the tree.

simplesect.para: the depth or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Whether the node has attributes.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if false, and -1 in case or error

briefdescription.para: Whether the node can have a text value.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if false, and -1 in case or error

briefdescription.para: Provides the text value of the node if present.

simplesect.para: the string or NULL if not available. The result must be deallocated with xmlFree

briefdescription.para: Provides the text value of the node if present.

simplesect.para: the string or NULL if not available. The result will be deallocated on the next Read() operation.

briefdescription.para: Whether an Attribute node was generated from the default value defined in the DTD or schema.

simplesect.para: 0 if not defaulted, 1 if defaulted, and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: The quotation mark character used to enclose the value of an attribute.

simplesect.para: " or ' and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: The xml:lang scope within which the node resides.

simplesect.para: the xml:lang value or NULL if none exists., if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: The xml:lang scope within which the node resides.

simplesect.para: the xml:lang value or NULL if none exists.

briefdescription.para: Get an interned string from the reader, allows for example to speedup string name comparisons.

simplesect.para: an interned copy of the string or NULL in case of error. The string will be deallocated with the reader.

briefdescription.para: The value indicating whether to normalize white space and attribute values.

detaileddescription.para: Since attribute value and end of line normalizations are a MUST in the XML specification only the value true is accepted. The broken behaviour of accepting out of range character entities like � is of course not supported either.

simplesect.para: 1 or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Change the parser processing behaviour by changing some of its internal properties.

detaileddescription.para: Note that some properties can only be changed before any read has been done.

simplesect.para: 0 if the call was successful, or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Read the parser internal property.

simplesect.para: the value, usually 0 or 1, or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Provide the line number of the current parsing point.

simplesect.para: an int or 0 if not available

briefdescription.para: Provide the column number of the current parsing point.

simplesect.para: an int or 0 if not available

briefdescription.para: Hacking interface allowing to get the xmlNode corresponding to the current node being accessed by the xmlTextReader.

detaileddescription.para: This is dangerous because the underlying node may be destroyed on the next Reads.

simplesect.para: the xmlNode or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: This tells the XML Reader to preserve the current node.

detaileddescription.para: The caller must also use xmlTextReaderCurrentDoc to keep an handle on the resulting document once parsing has finished

simplesect.para: the xmlNode or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: This tells the XML Reader to preserve all nodes matched by the pattern.

detaileddescription.para: The caller must also use xmlTextReaderCurrentDoc to keep an handle on the resulting document once parsing has finished

parameterdescription.para: the prefix definitions, array of [URI, prefix] or NULL

simplesect.para: a non-negative number in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Hacking interface allowing to get the xmlDoc corresponding to the current document being accessed by the xmlTextReader.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: as a result of this call, the reader will not destroy the associated XML document and calling xmlFreeDoc on the result is needed once the reader parsing has finished.

simplesect.para: the xmlDoc or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Use RelaxNG to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). if schema is NULL, then RelaxNG validation is deactivated. The schema should not be freed until the reader is deallocated or its use has been deactivated.

simplesect.para: 0 in case the RelaxNG validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Internal locator function for the readers.

simplesect.para: 0 in case the Schema validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Use XSD Schema to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). if schema is NULL, then Schema validation is deactivated. The schema should not be freed until the reader is deallocated or its use has been deactivated.

simplesect.para: 0 in case the Schema validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReaderPtr reader, const char *rng, xmlRelaxNGValidCtxtPtr ctxt, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlTextReaderRelaxNGValidateInternal

briefdescription.para: Use RelaxNG to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). If both rng and ctxt are NULL, then RelaxNG validation is deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: the path to a RelaxNG schema or NULL
the RelaxNG schema validation context or NULL

simplesect.para: 0 in case the RelaxNG validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReaderPtr reader, const char *xsd, xmlSchemaValidCtxtPtr ctxt, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlTextReaderSchemaValidateInternal

briefdescription.para: Validate the document as it is processed using XML Schema.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). If both xsd and ctxt are NULL then XML Schema validation is deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: the path to a W3C XSD schema or NULL
the XML Schema validation context or NULL

simplesect.para: 0 in case the schemas validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Use W3C XSD schema context to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). If ctxt is NULL, then XML Schema validation is deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: the XML Schema validation context or NULL

simplesect.para: 0 in case the schemas validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Use W3C XSD schema to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). If xsd is NULL, then XML Schema validation is deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: the path to a W3C XSD schema or NULL

simplesect.para: 0 in case the schemas validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Use RelaxNG schema context to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). If ctxt is NULL, then RelaxNG schema validation is deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: the RelaxNG schema validation context or NULL

simplesect.para: 0 in case the schemas validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Use RelaxNG schema to validate the document as it is processed.

detaileddescription.para: Activation is only possible before the first Read(). If rng is NULL, then RelaxNG schema validation is deactivated.

parameterdescription.para: the path to a RelaxNG schema or NULL

simplesect.para: 0 in case the schemas validation could be (de)activated and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Determine whether the current node is a namespace declaration rather than a regular attribute.

simplesect.para: 1 if the current node is a namespace declaration, 0 if it is a regular attribute or other type of node, or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Determine the XML version of the document being read.

simplesect.para: a string containing the XML version of the document or NULL in case of error. The string is deallocated with the reader.

briefdescription.para: Determine the standalone status of the document being read.

simplesect.para: 1 if the document was declared to be standalone, 0 if it was declared to be not standalone, or -1 if the document did not specify its standalone status or in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the line number for the given locator.

simplesect.para: the line number or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the base URI for the given locator.

simplesect.para: the base URI or NULL in case of error, if non NULL it need to be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Register a callback function that will be called on error and warnings.

detaileddescription.para: If f is NULL, the default error and warning handlers are restored.

parameterdescription.para: the callback function to call on error and warnings
a user argument to pass to the callback function

briefdescription.para: xmlTextReaderSetStructuredErrorHandler:

detaileddescription.para: Register a callback function that will be called on error and warnings.
If f is NULL, the default error and warning handlers are restored.

parameterdescription.para: the callback function to call on error and warnings
a user argument to pass to the callback function

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the error callback function and user argument.

parameterdescription.para: the callback function or NULL is no callback has been registered

briefdescription.para: Register a callback function that will be called to load external resources like entities.

parameterdescription.para: user data which will be passed to the loader

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the validity status from the parser context.

simplesect.para: the flag value 1 if valid, 0 if no, and -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReader *reader, xmlParserInputBuffer *input, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlTextReaderSetup

briefdescription.para: Setup an XML reader with new options.

parameterdescription.para: xmlParserInputBuffer used to feed the reader, will be destroyed with it.
the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Set the maximum amplification factor.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtSetMaxAmplification.

simplesect.para: the last error.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the current index of the parser used by the reader, relative to the start of the current entity.

detaileddescription.para: This function actually just wraps a call to xmlByteConsumed for the parser context associated with the reader. See xmlByteConsumed for more information.

xrefdescription.para: The returned value is mostly random and useless. It reflects the parser reading ahead and is in no way related to the current node.

simplesect.para: the index in bytes from the beginning of the entity or -1 in case the index could not be computed.

briefdescription.para: Create an xmltextReader for a preparsed document.

simplesect.para: the new reader or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlChar *cur, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderForDoc

briefdescription.para: Create an xmltextReader for an XML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string
the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the new reader or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: parse an XML file from the filesystem or the network.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption.

simplesect.para: the new reader or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *buffer, int size, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderForMemory

briefdescription.para: Create an xmltextReader for an XML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array
the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the new reader or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderForFd

briefdescription.para: Create an xmltextReader for an XML from a file descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption. NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the reader is closed or reset.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the new reader or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderForIO

briefdescription.para: Create an xmltextReader for an XML document from I/O functions and source.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the new reader or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Setup an xmltextReader to parse a preparsed XML document.

detaileddescription.para: This reuses the existing reader xmlTextReader.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReader *reader, const xmlChar *cur, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderNewDoc

briefdescription.para: Setup an xmltextReader to parse an XML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption. This reuses the existing reader xmlTextReader.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string
the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReader *reader, const char *filename, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderNewFile

briefdescription.para: parse an XML file from the filesystem or the network.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption. This reuses the existing reader xmlTextReader.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReader *reader, const char *buffer, int size, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderNewMemory

briefdescription.para: Setup an xmltextReader to parse an XML in-memory document.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption. This reuses the existing reader xmlTextReader.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array
the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReader *reader, int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderNewFd

briefdescription.para: Setup an xmltextReader to parse an XML from a file descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the reader is closed or reset. The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption. This reuses the existing reader xmlTextReader.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlTextReader *reader, xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReaderNewIO

briefdescription.para: Setup an xmltextReader to parse an XML document from I/O functions and source.

detaileddescription.para: The parsing flags options are a combination of xmlParserOption. This reuses the existing reader xmlTextReader.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error
